key_insight: The paper learns a fast neural proxy for sequential stochastic optimization so real-time order-fulfillment decisions can be made in a single forward pass instead of repeatedly solving an optimization model.
engineering_relevance: This enables sub-second, feasibility-aware decision support for omnichannel fulfillment under uncertainty, where latency and service quality both matter.
method: Train a scenario-embedded neural network offline on solver-generated C-SAA labels.
Use a decoder to enforce feasibility at inference time.
Optimize with a composite loss: imitation, constraint-violation penalty, and self-supervised cost alignment.
evidence_signal: In a JD.com-based calibrated simulator, the proxy is about 2800× faster than the online finite-sample C-SAA reference, lowers realized fulfillment cost by 3.3% versus that reference, and cuts total cost by at least 10.7% versus baseline policies.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and representativeness of the offline simulator/solver labels, so distribution shift or changing fulfillment dynamics could reduce reliability.
paper_title: Learning Optimization Proxies for Sequential Contextual Stochastic Programs: An Order Fulfillment Application
